text.title: ArgoCD GitOps Concepts
text.summary: Core resources (Application, Project, ApplicationSet), sync strategies, and hub-spoke vs distributed architecture.

list.item: Application: ArgoCD CR defining source Git repo/path/revision and destination cluster/namespace; the fundamental unit of deployment
AppProject: logical grouping with RBAC, allowed source repos, allowed destination clusters/namespaces; scope per team
ApplicationSet: template that generates multiple Applications from a generator (Git directory, matrix, cluster list, PR)

rule.statement: Never create Applications manually in the UI for production — manage all Application manifests in Git (App of Apps or ApplicationSet pattern).
rule.rationale: Manually created Applications cannot be version-controlled or reviewed. A deleted ArgoCD instance with manual Applications means starting from scratch.

rule.statement: Enable selfHeal: true on all environments to prevent configuration drift; use syncWindows to restrict automated syncs to maintenance windows for production.
rule.rationale: Without self-heal, a manual kubectl apply in production creates drift that is invisible until the next sync. Self-heal corrects this within seconds. syncWindows prevent ArgoCD from syncing production during incidents.

list.item: Development: automated: true, selfHeal: true, prune: true — maximum agility
Staging: automated: true, selfHeal: true, prune: true — same as dev
Production: automated: false (manual sync gate), selfHeal: true, prune: false (explicit prune confirmation)

example.code: spec: syncPolicy: automated: selfHeal: true prune: false # require explicit prune for resource deletion syncOptions: - CreateNamespace=true - ServerSideApply=true - ApplyOutOfSyncOnly=true retry: limit: 3 backoff: duration: 5s factor: 2 maxDuration: 3m

section.title: Architecture Patterns

rule.statement: Use folders to model environments, not branches — one repository with overlays/ for each environment is simpler than branch-per-environment.
rule.rationale: Branch-per-environment requires cherry-picking changes across branches and makes diff between environments difficult. Folder-per-environment shows environment differences as a clear Kustomize overlay diff.

list.item: Hub-and-spoke: single ArgoCD controls multiple clusters — single pane of glass, but network connectivity required and single point of failure
Distributed: ArgoCD per cluster — isolated failure domains but more instances to manage and no centralized view

antipattern.description: Storing Kubernetes Secrets directly in the GitOps repository, even base64-encoded.
antipattern.reason: Base64 is not encryption. Anyone with repo read access gets all credentials. Use External Secrets Operator, Sealed Secrets, or Vault integration instead.

section.reference: ArgoCD Application manifest with auto-sync and self-heal
